steps.title: Simplify the linear equation
steps.content:
Expand and simplify the second equation:
$$2y + 6 = 2(x + 3)$$
$$2y + 6 = 2x + 6$$
$$2y = 2x$$
$$y = x$$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for the positive root
steps.content:
Now we have two expressions for $y$:
  • From the first equation: $y = x^2$
  • From the simplified equation: $y = x$
Set them equal:
$$x^2 = x$$
$$x^2 - x = 0$$
$$x(x - 1) = 0$$
This gives $x = 0$ or $x = 1$. Since $x > 0$, we have $x = 1$.

Substituting back: $y = x = 1$, so $xy = 1 \cdot 1 = 1$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
